children.name: Eupetes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Malaysian Rail-babbler, one of the most enigmatic passerines in Southeast Asia. It has an exceptionally long neck, long legs, and a strikingly long tail, giving it a superficial resemblance to a rail. It is a skulking, ground-dwelling bird of the remote lowland forest floor, with a distinctive, far-carrying call that is far more frequently heard than the bird is seen.
